frameworks.name: neuromodulation of cortical tuning
frameworks.one_line: ACh and GABA(A) shape direction tuning and population coding in MT

frameworks.name: attentional gain modulation
frameworks.one_line: ACh effects compared against signatures of selective attention

frameworks.name: noise correlations and population coding
frameworks.one_line: pairwise correlations as a determinant of coding fidelity

core_question: How do cholinergic and GABAergic mechanisms differentially shape direction tuning, discriminability, reliability, and noise correlations in macaque area MT?
key_assumptions: ACh and gabazine effects can be isolated pharmacologically in MT
direction discriminability indexes coding quality of single neurons
simultaneously recorded pairs index population noise structure
manipulations: local application of acetylcholine vs gabazine in MT
measurement of direction tuning, discriminability, reliability, and pairwise correlations
key_findings: ACh increased firing rates without changing tuning width but improved direction discriminability
gabazine increased firing rates yet widened tuning curves and reduced discriminability
gabazine reduced response reliability while ACh had little effect on reliability
ACh decreased noise correlations whereas gabazine increased them
interpretations: ACh selectively improves neuronal tuning and coding in a manner consistent with attentional modulation, unlike GABA(A) blockade
